page: Amazon Web Services d2.xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor. This x86_64 architecture CPU provides 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 2.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 30.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 7.62 GB per core. A key hardware characteristic is its bundled local storage, which consists of 6144 GB of HDD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the d2.xlarge exhibits limited computational performance. Both single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks, including stress-ng and Geekbench, place the instance in the bottom 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth tests show weak performance in the bottom 25% for both read and write operations, although the PassMark memory score registers in the middle 50% average tier. Database operations and static web serving throughput also fall into the bottom 25% performance bracket.

Given its hardware profile, the d2.xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency primarily through its high local storage density rather than raw compute power. The massive 6.1 TB HDD capacity combined with moderate memory makes it suitable for storage-intensive workloads that do not demand high CPU throughput. Appropriate workloads include data warehousing, log processing, large-scale file systems, and MapReduce jobs where local storage volume is prioritized over high-speed computational performance.
description: Amazon Web Services d2.xlarge is a dense storage instance designed for workloads requiring significant local storage capacity. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor, this x86_64 system features 4 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 30.0 GB of system memory. It includes 6144 GB of local HDD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate that the instance has limited computational capabilities, with CPU and memory bandwidth tests generally scoring in the bottom 10% to 25% of systems. However, its PassMark memory score achieves an average rating. The d2.xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-dense applications where massive local capacity is required but high CPU performance is not critical. It is best suited for data warehousing, log analysis, and large-scale file systems.

tagline: A dense storage instance featuring massive local HDD capacity and moderate memory for storage-intensive data workloads.

6144 GB of bundled local HDD storage
